q1.question: What is your average individual score?
q1.answer: Average individual score for 18 is approximately 82.

q2.question: Strengths/weaknesses of golf game
q2.answer: Strengths - I am fairly consistent and usually (but not always) don't blow up on holes, so I can keep my individual scores the same most of the time.  Biggest weakness is lack of distance (based on age), which makes me a bad A player as I can't come anywhere close to the BIG hitters.

q3.question: How much silver do you own?
q3.answer: Not sure what silver we are referring to, but I don't invest in silver, gold or bitcoin.

q4.question: Describe your wiener using golf terms.
q4.answer: Short and hooking left.

q5.question: Why do you come on this trip?
q5.answer: Enjoy the golf and the young (yes, except for Fulk, Max and Rob, I am the old guy) friends I have made.  I love people who just have fun golfing no matter how they play and this group is that.  Plus this is a very welcoming group, so even though I am a fairly new edition to Camp, I never felt like an outsider.

q6.question: When do you plan on arriving to the course? (We can check in as early as Monday night if you're interested in getting moved in early. Plan is for first round of golf to be Tuesday afternoon.)
q6.answer: My plan is to get their early Wednesday.  Not sure if I can make the warm up round on that day (will try), but will be there for the official Wednesday afternoon round, and will stay through the Saturday morning round.  I wish I could add on to the trip, but have two other golf trips this year.

q1.question: What is your average individual score?
q1.answer: Average Individual score? 84-87

q2.question: Strengths/weaknesses of golf game
q2.answer:
Strengths: Distance and Putting. If I can get the driver working, we’re going to make some cash. Eagles should be in play. I’m pretty good at reading greens and finding the line. I’m a great pair with someone who can chip and find the fairway off the tee, so I can send it. 
Weakness: Chipping and Consistency: Chipping is hard, but I’m working on it. I’m learning a new driver and irons, so consistency is a bit off.

q3.answer: I own 69 x 0 troy oz of Ag

q5.question: Why do you come on this trip?
q5.answer: This is year 2 for me. I stumbled into this trip because my screen name is RumpleSmoothSkin. Nick couldn’t resist. I still fell like a rookie and I’m humbled to be invited back and made to feel like a welcome part of Golf Camp. We all work so hard to get through life. Trips like Golf Camp make it all worth to be alive and inspire us to enjoy the time we have. Much Love

q4.question: Describe your wiener using golf terms.
q4.answer: It's my driver, but I've heard it referred to as a smooth iron. It’s got a head that demands respect, a shaft that does most of the work, and a tendency to get extra stiff when the pressure’s on. Starts the round tucked neatly in the bag, but give it the right conditions and suddenly it’s out on the fairway, standing at full attention like it’s about to tee off at Augusta.
